evals.expected_output: Analysis: (1) hid.enumerate() returns all devices — opening by VID/PID will open the FIRST matching device; if two identical devices are connected, behavior is non-deterministic (use path instead); (2) The prepended 0x00 in write() is the report ID — correct for HID report 0, but should be documented; (3) No h.close() — handle leak if caller doesn't close; (4) No error handling for IOError from h.open() (device in use, permission denied on Linux without udev rules); (5) On Linux, hidraw requires udev rules or running as root.

assertions.text: Identifies that open(vid, pid) is non-deterministic with multiple identical devices (use path instead)
Explains the 0x00 prepend as the HID report ID
Flags the missing h.close() handle leak

evals.prompt: Our USB HID device works on Windows but on Linux we get 'Permission denied' when opening /dev/hidraw0. How do we fix this?
evals.expected_output: Linux udev rules solution: create /etc/udev/rules.d/99-myhid.rules with SUBSYSTEM=="hidraw", ATTRS{idVendor}=="<vid>", ATTRS{idProduct}=="<pid>", MODE="0666". Then run `udevadm control --reload-rules && udevadm trigger`. Also mention alternative of adding user to 'plugdev' group. Note that MODE=0666 gives world read/write — for production use GROUP="plugdev" MODE="0660" is safer.

assertions.text: Provides a udev rule solution with correct syntax
Mentions udevadm reload to activate rules without reboot
Notes security difference between MODE=0666 (world access) and plugdev group approach
